Executive Summary

Key Takeaways

  • Penn Station (97.0), Herald Square (96.0), and Times Square (96.0) are the Midtown revenue cluster — the richest event pipeline in the portfolio with 18, 21, and 34 qualifying events respectively. The BIG EAST Men’s Basketball Championship at MSG (Mar 14, score 10/10) is the single highest-impact event across all three stores simultaneously. Raising Cane’s (Competitor Pressure Score / CPS: 2,407 at 0.02mi) is Penn Station’s top competitive threat — its active loyalty promotions and direct adjacency demand priority digital counter-engagement. Herald Square faces Pelicana Chicken as an emerging high-CPS rival (1,009) at 0.02mi, a signal that Korean fried chicken is intensifying pressure in the 34th St corridor.
  • Flatiron (96.5 strength, 16 qualifying events) and Midtown Rock Center (95.0, 12 events) round out the core Manhattan cluster. Flatiron faces a dense 0.10mi concentration of Popeyes, Chipotle, McDonald’s, and Pollo Campero. Rock Center faces the lowest competitive CPS of any Manhattan store (McDonald’s at 0.26mi, CPS 36) — an unusually light promotional environment. Flamenco Fest is its standout upcoming cultural event. Terminal 5 pop concerts (bbno$, Artemas) create mid-week dinner demand across the Midtown/Herald Square corridor.
  • Downtown Brooklyn (86.0) is the strongest outer-borough location, with 13 qualifying events and a Barclays Center-anchored calendar. Brooklyn Nets home games score 9/10 throughout March, with Pre-Game Pass holders (100–300 early arrivals) providing an additional targeted pre-dinner window. Shake Shack at 0.01mi carries the highest CPS in the entire portfolio (4,868) — a direct, proximate threat that merits sustained counter-promotion. Community events including Caribeno Champs Classic and Reggae Fest broaden the audience beyond sports.
  • Harlem (84.3) and Financial District (94.5) show divergent competitive profiles. Harlem faces very high direct competitor threat (80/100) with KFC and Burger King at 0.02mi (CPS 1,581 and 1,382) — the most intensely competitive microenvironment in the portfolio. Financial District carries almost no competitive pressure (DCT 20/100, Shake Shack CPS 1,521 but at 0.02mi) and its high base score (94.5) reflects strong weekday lunch demand from the financial services office population. Neither store has currently mapped events above score 3 — supplemental event injection (St. Patrick’s Day parade, Spring Restaurant Week, Good Friday) provides the primary demand signal.
  • Upper East Side (92.4), Upper West Side (81.3), and Harlem collectively anchor the Upper Manhattan corridor. UES carries the highest net opportunity score in the portfolio (96.8) despite no currently mapped high-scoring events — its residential density and loyal repeat customer base support consistent baseline performance. UWS foot traffic proxy (37.7/100) is below its transit score (100/100), suggesting commuter-driven demand concentrated around subway exits on Columbus Ave. Both stores benefit from St. Patrick’s Day and Eid al-Fitr supplemental events, and from NYC Spring Restaurant Week (Mar 16–28).
  • The weather outlook is sharply bifurcated: Mar 10 is a prime window, Mar 12–14 and Mar 19–21 are sustained suppression periods. Mar 10 shows portfolio-wide WIS of +6.2 — the strongest positive signal in the entire forecast horizon. This coincides with the BIG EAST pre-tournament week, creating a compound uplift opportunity. Mar 12–14 (WIS −5.3 to −6.1) is the critical risk window, directly overlapping the BIG EAST Championship weekend at MSG — a rare scenario where the highest-impact event calendar and the worst weather conditions collide simultaneously across the Midtown cluster. Mar 19–21 shows a second suppression band (WIS −5.9 to −6.0). Mar 23 is the single worst forecast day across the portfolio (WIS −6.7).
  • Fordham (74.8) and Elmhurst (66.7) serve distinct community anchor roles. Fordham’s Bronx location has the lowest competitive CPS density after filtering but no high-scoring local events in the current window — citywide supplementals (St. Patrick’s Day, Spring Break) are the primary opportunity drivers. Elmhurst faces the most competitive promo environment of any store by count (19 competitors after filtering), led by Chipotle at 0.01mi (CPS 3,418) — the highest-density multi-brand QSR cluster in the portfolio. Eid al-Fitr (Mar 20) is Elmhurst’s single highest-relevance supplemental event, scoring 7/10 given its large South/Southeast Asian Muslim community.
  • New Springville (39.9) is the portfolio’s car-dependent outlier with limited event upside but low competitive noise. Transit connectivity scores 0/100 (Staten Island Mall is auto-access only), and tourism is near-zero. However, competitive threat is also the lowest in the portfolio (DCT 20, Popeyes at 0.25mi CPS 63). Easter weekend and Spring Break represent the primary demand windows. The store’s consistent mall-anchor foot traffic provides stable baseline volume even in the absence of event-driven surges.
🍗

★ Top Event
St. Patrick's Day Parade
Mar 17, 2026 · 11:00 · 5th Ave / Times Square Corridor
Impact: 8/10 All-Day Cap: 2,000,000 Parade
The St. Patrick's Day Parade starts at 44th St and 5th Ave, running directly through the Times Square corridor. Massive spectator crowds plus the usual Times Square tourism create a very high all-day foot traffic surge. One of the highest-demand days of the year.
BIG EAST Men's Basketball Tournament Session 5: Championship Game7/10Affinity: Very HighSports
Mar 14, 2026 · 18:30 · Madison Square Garden · 0.60mi · Dinner
The event at Madison Square Garden has a high impact on demand due to its large venue capacity of about 20,000 and an expected attendance range of 8,800 to 19,600, indicating a major crowd draw of sports fans likely to dine before or after the game; however, actual turnout may be lower than capacity, creating some uncertainty in demand levels.
BIG EAST Men's Basketball Tournament Session 4: Semifinal Doubleheader6/10Affinity: Very HighSports
Mar 13, 2026 · 17:30 · Madison Square Garden · 0.60mi · Dinner
The event at Madison Square Garden has a high impact on demand because it can attract between 8,800 and 19,600 attendees, all within a short travel distance of the Times Square store, during the dinner meal window when sports fans are likely to eat before or after the games; however, the venue capacity of about 20,000 sets an upper bound and actual turnout may be much lower.

Mar 27, 2026 · 19:00 · Madison Square Garden · 0.60mi · Dinner

Although the venue capacity is about 20,000, actual turnout may be lower, but the close proximity of the store (0.6 miles) and the timing on a weekday evening support strong foot traffic and increased dining demand.
Spring Break (NYC Schools)6/10
Apr 02, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
NYC public school spring break increases family dining demand across all neighbourhood locations. More families active mid-day creates above-average lunch and afternoon traffic for the full week.

Mar 19, 2026 · 20:00 · Madison Square Garden · 0.60mi · Dinner
Lady Gaga: The MAYHEM Ball is a sold-out global arena tour at Madison Square Garden (capacity ~20,000). Expected attendance is 18,000–20,000 Despite the distance penalty, the sheer volume of attendees flowing through Midtown creates significant dinner-hour foot traffic uplift for stores within walking distance.

Good Friday5/10
Apr 03, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
Good Friday sees many office closures, school breaks, and early dismissals across NYC. Midday and afternoon dining demand is elevated as residents and families are home or in retail areas rather than at work.
Dervish5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 11, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.20mi · Dinner
Dervish is an acclaimed Irish traditional music band with a dedicated and passionate fanbase. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) fills to 60–85% for established folk and Celtic acts, putting expected attendance at ~1,200–1,700 Pre-show dinner demand is genuine given the dinner timing.
Fia w/ special guest Johnny Suite5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 22:00 · Sony Hall · 0.20mi
Fia is an emerging artist; Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is unlikely to sell out for this booking. Realistic attendance is ~800–1,300 (40–65% fill) Pre-show dinner opportunity exists but is more moderate than for a sold-out headline act.
Dancing With The Celts5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 16, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.20mi · Dinner
Dancing With The Celts is a Celtic dance and music show with broad appeal to both Irish diaspora and tourist audiences in Midtown. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) typically fills to 50–80% for this type of cultural show, putting expected attendance at ~1,000–1,600. Dinner timing creates a genuine pre-show dining opportunity nearby.
Altan w/ special guest Alison Brown5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 17, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.20mi · Dinner
Altan is one of Ireland’s most celebrated traditional music bands with a loyal international following. Alison Brown is a respected bluegrass artist. Together, this is a strong bill for the folk/roots audience. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is expected to fill to ~1,000–1,500 (50–75%). Pre-show dinner demand is meaningful given the dinner timing.
Noize MC - Not All There Tour5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 21, 2026 · 21:00 · Palladium Times Square · 0.20mi
Noize MC is a prominent Russian alternative hip-hop artist with a substantial following among the Russian-speaking diaspora in New York City. Palladium Times Square (capacity ~3,000) is expected to draw ~1,200–2,000 attendees — The audience skews young and food-motivated; meaningful pre-show dinner demand around the venue.
The Surfrajettes w/ Bethlehem Shalom5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 26, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.20mi · Dinner
The Surfrajettes are a rising surf/rock act with a growing following. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is unlikely to fill to capacity for this booking; realistic attendance is ~800–1,300 (40–65% fill). Pre-show dinner demand is moderate given the dinner timing and audience size.
Reik Tour 20265/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 13, 2026 · 20:00 · Infosys Theater at Madison Square Garden · 0.60mi · Dinner
Reik Tour plays the Hulu Theater at MSG (capacity ~5,600), not the main arena. A Latin pop headline tour at this venue typically sells 3,500–5,000 tickets. The distance penalty from MSG to the Times Square store applies, but the volume of attendees creates measurable pre-show dinner demand.
FKA twigs Body High Tour: A live show with FKA twigs w/ Special Guests5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 21, 2026 · 20:00 · Madison Square Garden · 0.60mi · Dinner
FKA twigs Body High Tour at Madison Square Garden (capacity ~20,000) is a major booking. Expected attendance is ~10,000–15,000 based on her fanbase scale and MSG floor/lower bowl configuration. The distance penalty from MSG applies, but the event generates meaningful Midtown pre-show dinner traffic.
Orrin Evans Trio4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 20, 2026 · 19:00 · Birdland Theater · 0.10mi · Dinner
Birdland Theater (capacity ~300) is a fully ticketed, reservation-required jazz venue that typically runs at 70–90% capacity. Orrin Evans is a well-regarded jazz pianist with a dedicated following — expected attendance is ~200–270. As a dinner-jazz venue, most Birdland Theater attendees dine inside the club, limiting foot-traffic spillover to nearby Chick-fil-A stores despite close proximity and dinner timing.
Orleans4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 20, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.20mi · Dinner
Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is unlikely to fill to capacity for Orleans, a classic rock nostalgia act with a loyal but niche fanbase. Realistic attendance is ~600–900 (30–45% fill)but below the crowd volumes seen for mainstream pop or hip-hop bookings at the same venue. Pre-show dinner demand exists but is moderate given audience size and demographic.
Hieroglyphics | Casual • Domino • Pep Love • DJ Toure • Souls of Mischief • Del The Funky Homosapien4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 21, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.20mi · Dinner
Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) typically fills to 60–90% for ticketed hip-hop shows. Hieroglyphics is a celebrated West Coast hip-hop collective headlining with Souls of Mischief, Del the Funky Homosapien and others — expected attendance is ~1,200–1,800 The hip-hop audience skews young and food-motivated; pre-show dinner demand around Sony Hall is genuine.
BIG EAST Men's Basketball Tournament Session 1: First Round Triplehedr4/10Affinity: Very HighSports
Mar 11, 2026 · 16:00 · Madison Square Garden · 0.60mi
The BIG EAST First Round Tripleheader at Madison Square Garden (capacity ~20,000) draws ~10,000–15,000 college basketball fans across three back-to-back games Score is 4/10 for the Times Square store: the distance penalty from MSG applies (~0.8mi), and the early session start means the dinner meal window bonus does not fully activate. Nonetheless, the volume of fans moving through Midtown throughout the day creates a meaningful background uplift in foot traffic.
Eid al-Fitr4/10
Mar 20, 2026 · 08:00 · Citywide · Lunch
Eid al-Fitr brings citywide family dining demand to Midtown. Times Square draws large tourist and commuter volumes year-round; on Eid, Muslim families and community groups travelling through the area add meaningful mid-day dining demand at and around the store.
NYC Spring Restaurant Week4/10
Mar 16, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide / Manhattan · Lunch
NYC Restaurant Week (Mar 16–28) drives heightened dining consideration across the city. While high-end restaurants see the direct benefit, the heightened dining mindset and increased foot traffic in commercial areas creates an above-baseline halo effect for all nearby QSR options — particularly for lunch and post-work dinner.
The High Kings - Rocky Road to Dublin - 2026 Tour4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 13, 2026 · 20:00 · Town Hall · 0.30mi · Dinner
The High Kings are an established Irish folk group with a loyal fanbase. Town Hall (capacity ~1,500) typically fills to 60–90% for ticketed folk and Celtic shows, putting expected attendance at ~900–1,350 Pre-show dinner demand is genuine, particularly during the dinner meal window.
Celebrating Sabicas Presented By Flamenco Fest & World Music Institute4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 20:00 · Town Hall · 0.30mi · Dinner
Flamenco Fest is a well-regarded annual festival, but flamenco draws a niche, specialist audience. Town Hall (capacity ~1,500) is unlikely to fill fully for this presentation; realistic attendance is ~600–1,000 (40–67% fill) but below mainstream acts at the same venue. Audience demographic skews culturally engaged adults; moderate pre-show dining demand.
bbno$ (16 and Over)4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 13, 2026 · 20:00 · Terminal 5 · 0.60mi · Dinner
Terminal 5 (capacity ~3,000) regularly sells out or near-sells out for popular acts. bbno$ draws a young, energetic crowd — expected attendance 1,500–2,500. Strong pre- and post-show dining demand for the nearby Chick-fil-A, particularly during the dinner window.
Artemas (16+)4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 20:00 · Terminal 5 · 0.60mi · Dinner
Terminal 5 (capacity ~3,000) typically fills to 1,500–2,500 for Artemas, a rising pop act with a young, food-motivated fanbase. Strong pre-show dinner opportunity given venue proximity and early evening start.
Anat Cohen Quartet3/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 10, 2026 · 19:00 · Birdland Jazz Club · 0.10mi · Dinner
Birdland is a fully ticketed, reservation-required jazz venue. The 300-seat Birdland Jazz Club typically runs at 70–90% capacity, with expected attendance of ~200–280 per show. As a dinner-jazz venue, most attendees dine inside the club, limiting foot-traffic spillover to the nearby Chick-fil-A despite close proximity and dinner timing. The jazz audience demographic also skews toward in-venue dining over QSR options.

St. Patrick's Day Parade8/10Parade
Mar 17, 2026 · 11:00 · 5th Ave / 34th–44th St · All-Day
The St. Patrick's Day Parade marches up 5th Ave starting from 44th St, just two blocks from Herald Square. With over 150,000 marchers and up to 2 million spectators lining the route, the area sees one of the biggest all-day dining surges of the year. Strong demand from brunch through dinner.

Spring Break (NYC Schools)6/10
Apr 02, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
NYC public school spring break increases family dining demand across all neighbourhood locations. More families active mid-day creates above-average lunch and afternoon traffic for the full week.
Good Friday5/10
Apr 03, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
Good Friday sees many office closures, school breaks, and early dismissals across NYC. Midday and afternoon dining demand is elevated as residents and families are home or in retail areas rather than at work.
Noize MC - Not All There Tour5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 21, 2026 · 21:00 · Palladium Times Square · 0.50mi
Noize MC is a prominent Russian alternative hip-hop artist with a substantial following among the Russian-speaking diaspora in New York City. Palladium Times Square (capacity ~3,000) is expected to draw ~1,200–2,000 attendees — The audience skews young and food-motivated; meaningful pre-show dinner demand around the venue.
Dervish5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 11, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.60mi · Dinner
Dervish is an acclaimed Irish traditional music band with a dedicated and passionate fanbase. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) fills to 60–85% for established folk and Celtic acts, putting expected attendance at ~1,200–1,700 Pre-show dinner demand is genuine given the dinner timing.
Fia w/ special guest Johnny Suite5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 22:00 · Sony Hall · 0.60mi
Fia is an emerging artist; Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is unlikely to sell out for this booking. Realistic attendance is ~800–1,300 (40–65% fill) Pre-show dinner opportunity exists but is more moderate than for a sold-out headline act.
Dancing With The Celts5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 16, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.60mi · Dinner
Dancing With The Celts is a Celtic dance and music show with broad appeal to both Irish diaspora and tourist audiences in Midtown. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) typically fills to 50–80% for this type of cultural show, putting expected attendance at ~1,000–1,600. Dinner timing creates a genuine pre-show dining opportunity nearby.
Altan w/ special guest Alison Brown5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 17, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.60mi · Dinner
Altan is one of Ireland’s most celebrated traditional music bands with a loyal international following. Alison Brown is a respected bluegrass artist. Together, this is a strong bill for the folk/roots audience. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is expected to fill to ~1,000–1,500 (50–75%). Pre-show dinner demand is meaningful given the dinner timing.
The Surfrajettes w/ Bethlehem Shalom5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 26, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.60mi · Dinner
The Surfrajettes are a rising surf/rock act with a growing following. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is unlikely to fill to capacity for this booking; realistic attendance is ~800–1,300 (40–65% fill). Pre-show dinner demand is moderate given the dinner timing and audience size.
TJ Monterde & KZ Tandingan - In Between4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 19:00 · Palladium Times Square · 0.50mi · Dinner
TJ Monterde & KZ Tandingan are hugely popular Filipino pop artists with a large and passionate diaspora fanbase in New York City. Palladium Times Square (capacity ~3,000) is expected to fill to ~1,800–2,500 The audience skews young and highly food-motivated; strong pre-show dinner demand around the venue.
Eid al-Fitr4/10
Mar 20, 2026 · 08:00 · Citywide · Lunch
Eid al-Fitr brings citywide family dining demand, with Herald Square area benefiting from mid-day crowds and post-prayer family groups. Above-average lunch and afternoon traffic expected.
NYC Spring Restaurant Week4/10
Mar 16, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide / Manhattan · Lunch
NYC Restaurant Week (Mar 16–28) drives heightened dining consideration across the city. While high-end restaurants see the direct benefit, the heightened dining mindset and increased foot traffic in commercial areas creates an above-baseline halo effect for all nearby QSR options — particularly for lunch and post-work dinner.
The High Kings - Rocky Road to Dublin - 2026 Tour4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 13, 2026 · 20:00 · Town Hall · 0.50mi · Dinner
The High Kings are an established Irish folk group with a loyal fanbase. Town Hall (capacity ~1,500) typically fills to 60–90% for ticketed folk and Celtic shows, putting expected attendance at ~900–1,350 Pre-show dinner demand is genuine, particularly during the dinner meal window.
Celebrating Sabicas Presented By Flamenco Fest & World Music Institute4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 20:00 · Town Hall · 0.50mi · Dinner
Flamenco Fest is a well-regarded annual festival, but flamenco draws a niche, specialist audience. Town Hall (capacity ~1,500) is unlikely to fill fully for this presentation; realistic attendance is ~600–1,000 (40–67% fill) but below mainstream acts at the same venue. Audience demographic skews culturally engaged adults; moderate pre-show dining demand.
bbno$ (16 and Over)4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 13, 2026 · 20:00 · Terminal 5 · 1.00mi · Dinner
Terminal 5 (capacity ~3,000) regularly sells out or near-sells out for popular acts. bbno$ draws a young, energetic crowd — expected attendance 1,500–2,500. Strong pre- and post-show dining demand for the nearby Chick-fil-A, particularly during the dinner window.
Artemas (16+)4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 20:00 · Terminal 5 · 1.00mi · Dinner
Terminal 5 (capacity ~3,000) typically fills to 1,500–2,500 for Artemas, a rising pop act with a young, food-motivated fanbase. Strong pre-show dinner opportunity given venue proximity and early evening start.

Mar 21, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.60mi · Dinner

Hieroglyphics is a celebrated West Coast hip-hop collective headlining with Souls of Mischief, Del the Funky Homosapien and others — expected attendance is ~1,200–1,800 The hip-hop audience skews young and food-motivated; pre-show dinner demand around Sony Hall is genuine.

St. Patrick's Day Parade6/10Parade
Mar 17, 2026 · 11:00 · Citywide / Penn Station Transit Hub · All-Day
St. Patrick's Day generates citywide transit surges through Penn Station as visitors arrive for the 5th Ave parade. Post-parade crowds dispersing through Midtown create elevated dinner demand throughout the area.
Spring Break (NYC Schools)6/10
Apr 02, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
NYC public school spring break increases family dining demand across all neighbourhood locations. More families active mid-day creates above-average lunch and afternoon traffic for the full week.
Good Friday5/10
Apr 03, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
Good Friday sees many office closures, school breaks, and early dismissals across NYC. Midday and afternoon dining demand is elevated as residents and families are home or in retail areas rather than at work.
Noize MC - Not All There Tour5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 21, 2026 · 21:00 · Palladium Times Square · 0.50mi
Noize MC is a prominent Russian alternative hip-hop artist with a substantial following among the Russian-speaking diaspora in New York City. Palladium Times Square (capacity ~3,000) is expected to draw ~1,200–2,000 attendees — The audience skews young and food-motivated; meaningful pre-show dinner demand around the venue.
Dervish5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 11, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.60mi · Dinner
Dervish is an acclaimed Irish traditional music band with a dedicated and passionate fanbase. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) fills to 60–85% for established folk and Celtic acts, putting expected attendance at ~1,200–1,700 Pre-show dinner demand is genuine given the dinner timing.
Fia w/ special guest Johnny Suite5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 22:00 · Sony Hall · 0.60mi
Fia is an emerging artist; Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is unlikely to sell out for this booking. Realistic attendance is ~800–1,300 (40–65% fill) Pre-show dinner opportunity exists but is more moderate than for a sold-out headline act.
Dancing With The Celts5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 16, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.60mi · Dinner
Dancing With The Celts is a Celtic dance and music show with broad appeal to both Irish diaspora and tourist audiences in Midtown. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) typically fills to 50–80% for this type of cultural show, putting expected attendance at ~1,000–1,600. Dinner timing creates a genuine pre-show dining opportunity nearby.
Altan w/ special guest Alison Brown5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 17, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.60mi · Dinner
Altan is one of Ireland’s most celebrated traditional music bands with a loyal international following. Alison Brown is a respected bluegrass artist. Together, this is a strong bill for the folk/roots audience. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is expected to fill to ~1,000–1,500 (50–75%). Pre-show dinner demand is meaningful given the dinner timing.
The Surfrajettes w/ Bethlehem Shalom5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 26, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.60mi · Dinner
The Surfrajettes are a rising surf/rock act with a growing following. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is unlikely to fill to capacity for this booking; realistic attendance is ~800–1,300 (40–65% fill). Pre-show dinner demand is moderate given the dinner timing and audience size.
Eid al-Fitr4/10
Mar 20, 2026 · 08:00 · Citywide · Lunch
Eid al-Fitr generates elevated family dining demand across Midtown. Penn Station serves as a transit hub for Muslim community members travelling to prayers and family gatherings, with dining demand elevated mid-morning through afternoon.
NYC Spring Restaurant Week4/10
Mar 16, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide / Manhattan · Lunch
NYC Restaurant Week (Mar 16–28) drives heightened dining consideration across the city. While high-end restaurants see the direct benefit, the heightened dining mindset and increased foot traffic in commercial areas creates an above-baseline halo effect for all nearby QSR options — particularly for lunch and post-work dinner.
The High Kings - Rocky Road to Dublin - 2026 Tour4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 13, 2026 · 20:00 · Town Hall · 0.50mi · Dinner
The High Kings are an established Irish folk group with a loyal fanbase. Town Hall (capacity ~1,500) typically fills to 60–90% for ticketed folk and Celtic shows, putting expected attendance at ~900–1,350 Pre-show dinner demand is genuine, particularly during the dinner meal window.
Celebrating Sabicas Presented By Flamenco Fest & World Music Institute4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 20:00 · Town Hall · 0.50mi · Dinner
Flamenco Fest is a well-regarded annual festival, but flamenco draws a niche, specialist audience. Town Hall (capacity ~1,500) is unlikely to fill fully for this presentation; realistic attendance is ~600–1,000 (40–67% fill) but below mainstream acts at the same venue. Audience demographic skews culturally engaged adults; moderate pre-show dining demand.

Mar 14, 2026 · 19:00 · Palladium Times Square · 0.50mi · Dinner
TJ Monterde & KZ Tandingan are hugely popular Filipino pop artists with a large and passionate diaspora fanbase in New York City. Palladium Times Square (capacity ~3,000) is expected to fill to ~1,800–2,500 The audience skews young and highly food-motivated; strong pre-show dinner demand around the venue.

St. Patrick's Day Parade
Mar 17, 2026 · 11:00 · 5th Ave at Rockefeller Center
Impact: 8/10 All-Day Cap: 2,000,000 Parade
The St. Patrick's Day Parade runs up 5th Ave past Rockefeller Center, with the parade route adjacent to this store. An estimated 2 million spectators line the route, with Rock Center itself a major gathering point. All-day demand is very high.

Noize MC - Not All There Tour5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 21, 2026 · 21:00 · Palladium Times Square · 0.20mi
Noize MC is a prominent Russian alternative hip-hop artist with a substantial following among the Russian-speaking diaspora in New York City. Palladium Times Square (capacity ~3,000) is expected to draw ~1,200–2,000 attendees — The audience skews young and food-motivated; meaningful pre-show dinner demand around the venue.
Dervish5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 11, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.30mi · Dinner
Dervish is an acclaimed Irish traditional music band with a dedicated and passionate fanbase. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) fills to 60–85% for established folk and Celtic acts, putting expected attendance at ~1,200–1,700 Pre-show dinner demand is genuine given the dinner timing.
Fia w/ special guest Johnny Suite5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 22:00 · Sony Hall · 0.30mi
Fia is an emerging artist; Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is unlikely to sell out for this booking. Realistic attendance is ~800–1,300 (40–65% fill) Pre-show dinner opportunity exists but is more moderate than for a sold-out headline act.
Dancing With The Celts5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 16, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.30mi · Dinner
Dancing With The Celts is a Celtic dance and music show with broad appeal to both Irish diaspora and tourist audiences in Midtown. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) typically fills to 50–80% for this type of cultural show, putting expected attendance at ~1,000–1,600. Dinner timing creates a genuine pre-show dining opportunity nearby.
Altan w/ special guest Alison Brown5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 17, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.30mi · Dinner
Altan is one of Ireland’s most celebrated traditional music bands with a loyal international following. Alison Brown is a respected bluegrass artist. Together, this is a strong bill for the folk/roots audience. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is expected to fill to ~1,000–1,500 (50–75%). Pre-show dinner demand is meaningful given the dinner timing.
The Surfrajettes w/ Bethlehem Shalom5/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 26, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.30mi · Dinner
The Surfrajettes are a rising surf/rock act with a growing following. Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is unlikely to fill to capacity for this booking; realistic attendance is ~800–1,300 (40–65% fill). Pre-show dinner demand is moderate given the dinner timing and audience size.
Celebrating Sabicas Presented By Flamenco Fest & World Music Institute4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 20:00 · Town Hall · 0.20mi · Dinner
Flamenco Fest is a well-regarded annual festival, but flamenco draws a niche, specialist audience. Town Hall (capacity ~1,500) is unlikely to fill fully for this presentation; realistic attendance is ~600–1,000 (40–67% fill) but below mainstream acts at the same venue. Audience demographic skews culturally engaged adults; moderate pre-show dining demand.
TJ Monterde & KZ Tandingan - In Between4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 19:00 · Palladium Times Square · 0.20mi · Dinner
TJ Monterde & KZ Tandingan are hugely popular Filipino pop artists with a large and passionate diaspora fanbase in New York City. Palladium Times Square (capacity ~3,000) is expected to fill to ~1,800–2,500 The audience skews young and highly food-motivated; strong pre-show dinner demand around the venue.
Hieroglyphics | Casual • Domino • Pep Love • DJ Toure • Souls of Mischief • Del The Funky Homosapien4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 21, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.30mi · Dinner
Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) typically fills to 60–90% for ticketed hip-hop shows. Hieroglyphics is a celebrated West Coast hip-hop collective headlining with Souls of Mischief, Del the Funky Homosapien and others — expected attendance is ~1,200–1,800 The hip-hop audience skews young and food-motivated; pre-show dinner demand around Sony Hall is genuine.

Mar 16, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide / Manhattan · Lunch
NYC Restaurant Week (Mar 16–28) drives heightened dining consideration across the city. While high-end restaurants see the direct benefit, the heightened dining mindset and increased foot traffic in commercial areas creates an above-baseline halo effect for all nearby QSR options — particularly for lunch and post-work dinner.
The High Kings - Rocky Road to Dublin - 2026 Tour4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 13, 2026 · 20:00 · Town Hall · 0.20mi · Dinner
The High Kings are an established Irish folk group with a loyal fanbase. Town Hall (capacity ~1,500) typically fills to 60–90% for ticketed folk and Celtic shows, putting expected attendance at ~900–1,350 Pre-show dinner demand is genuine, particularly during the dinner meal window.
bbno$ (16 and Over)4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 13, 2026 · 20:00 · Terminal 5 · 0.60mi · Dinner
Terminal 5 (capacity ~3,000) regularly sells out or near-sells out for popular acts. bbno$ draws a young, energetic crowd — expected attendance 1,500–2,500. Strong pre- and post-show dining demand for the nearby Chick-fil-A, particularly during the dinner window.
Artemas (16+)4/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 20:00 · Terminal 5 · 0.60mi · Dinner
Terminal 5 (capacity ~3,000) typically fills to 1,500–2,500 for Artemas, a rising pop act with a young, food-motivated fanbase. Strong pre-show dinner opportunity given venue proximity and early evening start.
Mae Martin: The Possum3/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 26, 2026 · 19:00 · Town Hall · 0.20mi · Dinner
Mae Martin is a well-known comedian, writer and actor with a strong following in NYC, particularly among younger and LGBTQ+ audiences. Town Hall (capacity ~1,500) fills well for established comedy acts; expected attendance is ~900–1,350 Comedy audiences are typically food-motivated before shows; solid pre-show dinner demand.
Orleans3/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 20, 2026 · 20:00 · Sony Hall · 0.30mi · Dinner
Sony Hall (capacity ~2,000) is unlikely to fill to capacity for Orleans, a classic rock nostalgia act with a loyal but niche fanbase. Realistic attendance is ~600–900 (30–45% fill)but below the crowd volumes seen for mainstream pop or hip-hop bookings at the same venue. Pre-show dinner demand exists but is moderate given audience size and demographic.

Brooklyn Nets v. Memphis Grizzlies
Mar 09, 2026 · 19:30 · Barclays Center · 0.10mi away
Impact: 9/10 Dinner Cap: 17,732 Affinity: Very High Sports
The event at Barclays Center has a very high impact on demand because it draws a major crowd of 7,800 to 17,400 sports fans who are likely to dine before or after the game, with the venue capacity of about 17,700 setting an upper limit; given the store's very close proximity, this results in significantly increased foot traffic during the dinner period despite some uncertainty in actual turnout.
Brooklyn Nets v. Detroit Pistons (West Indian Carnival) 9/10Affinity: Very HighSports
Mar 10, 2026 · 19:30 · Barclays Center · 0.10mi · Dinner
The event at Barclays Center, with a venue capacity of about 17.7k and an expected attendance between 7.8k and 17.4k, is very close to the Downtown Brooklyn store and occurs during the dinner meal window on a weekday, leading to very high demand as sports fans typically eat before or after games; although the venue capacity sets an upper bound, actual turnout may vary but is still expected to be substantial.
Caribeno Champs Classic9/10Affinity: Very HighSports
Mar 13, 2026 · 18:30 · Barclays Center · 0.10mi · Dinner
The event at Barclays Center, with a venue capacity of about 15,000 and expected attendance between 6,600 and 14,700, is likely to generate very high demand for dinner as sports fans typically eat before or after games; the store's very close proximity further supports strong foot traffic, though actual turnout may be lower than capacity.
Brooklyn Nets v. Portland Trail Blazers9/10Affinity: Very HighSports
Mar 16, 2026 · 19:30 · Barclays Center · 0.10mi · Dinner
The Brooklyn Nets game at Barclays Center, with a venue capacity of about 17.7k and expected attendance between 7.8k and 17.4k, is a very high demand event because sports fans typically seek dining options nearby before or after the game, especially during the dinner window; although the venue capacity sets an upper limit, actual turnout may vary but is still likely substantial given the event's proximity and timing.
Brooklyn Nets v. Oklahoma City Thunder (Women's Impact Night)9/10Affinity: Very HighSports
Mar 18, 2026 · 19:30 · Barclays Center · 0.10mi · Dinner
The event at Barclays Center, with a venue capacity of about 17.7k and expected attendance between 7.8k and 17.4k, is likely to generate very high demand for dinner as sports fans typically eat before or after games; the store's very close proximity (0.1 miles) further supports strong foot traffic, though actual turnout may be lower than capacity.
Brooklyn Nets v. New York Knicks9/10Affinity: Very HighSports
Mar 20, 2026 · 19:30 · Barclays Center · 0.10mi · Dinner
The Brooklyn Nets vs. New York Knicks game at Barclays Center, with a venue capacity of about 17.7k and expected attendance between 7.8k and 17.4k, is likely to generate very high dining demand due to its proximity to the Downtown Brooklyn store and the typical behavior of sports fans eating before or after evening games, though actual turnout may be lower than full capacity.
New Edition: The New Edition Way Tour7/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 14, 2026 · 20:00 · Barclays Center · 0.10mi · Dinner
The concert at Barclays Center, with a venue capacity of about 17.7k and an expected attendance between 6k and 15.6k, is likely to generate high foot traffic near the Downtown Brooklyn Chick-fil-A during the dinner window, as concert-goers often seek meals before or after the show; however, actual turnout may be lower than capacity, introducing some uncertainty.
Reggae Fest Presents Alkaline7/10Affinity: HighConcert
Mar 21, 2026 · 20:00 · Barclays Center · 0.10mi · Dinner
The event at Barclays Center, with a venue capacity of about 17.7k and expected attendance between 6k and 15.6k, is likely to generate high foot traffic near the Downtown Brooklyn store due to its close proximity and timing during the dinner meal window on a weekend, attracting concert-goers who typically seek meals around show times; however, actual turnout may vary significantly below venue capacity.
Spring Break (NYC Schools)6/10
Apr 02, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
NYC public school spring break increases family dining demand across all neighbourhood locations. More families active mid-day creates above-average lunch and afternoon traffic for the full week.
Eid al-Fitr5/10
Mar 20, 2026 · 08:00 · Brooklyn · All-Day
Downtown Brooklyn is adjacent to significant Muslim and Caribbean-Muslim communities in Flatbush and Bed-Stuy. Eid al-Fitr drives elevated family dining demand in the area throughout the day.
Good Friday5/10
Apr 03, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
Good Friday sees many office closures, school breaks, and early dismissals across NYC. Midday and afternoon dining demand is elevated as residents and families are home or in retail areas rather than at work.
St. Patrick's Day Parade4/10Parade
Mar 17, 2026 · 14:00 · Downtown Brooklyn · All-Day
St. Patrick's Day drives elevated dining demand in Downtown Brooklyn through general city festivity and spillover from Manhattan. Evening dining and bar traffic is notably above baseline.
NYC Spring Restaurant Week4/10
Mar 16, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide / Manhattan · Lunch
NYC Restaurant Week (Mar 16–28) drives heightened dining consideration across the city. While high-end restaurants see the direct benefit, the heightened dining mindset and increased foot traffic in commercial areas creates an above-baseline halo effect for all nearby QSR options — particularly for lunch and post-work dinner.
Brooklyn Nets v. Memphis Grizzlies : Pre-Game Pass (Watch Warm-Ups)4/10Affinity: HighFan Experience
Mar 09, 2026 · 19:30 · Barclays Center · 0.10mi · Dinner
Barclays Center capacity is 17,732, but Pre-Game Pass holders (100–300 people) access the floor for warm-ups ~90 minutes before tip-off. These are highly engaged Nets fans arriving early — a targeted pre-game dinner opportunity for the adjacent Chick-fil-A. Impact is moderate given the limited pass-holder count versus a full-capacity game.

Eid al-Fitr
Mar 20, 2026 · 08:00 · Harlem / East Harlem
Impact: 6/10 All-Day
Harlem has a significant Muslim population, and Eid al-Fitr is one of the most important community days of the year. Post-prayer family meals and community gatherings drive strong all-day dining demand, particularly for halal-friendly options in the neighbourhood.
Spring Break (NYC Schools)6/10
Apr 02, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
NYC public school spring break increases family dining demand across all neighbourhood locations. More families active mid-day creates above-average lunch and afternoon traffic for the full week.
Good Friday5/10
Apr 03, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
Good Friday sees many office closures, school breaks, and early dismissals across NYC. Midday and afternoon dining demand is elevated as residents and families are home or in retail areas rather than at work.
St. Patrick's Day Parade4/10Parade
Mar 17, 2026 · 12:00 · Harlem · All-Day
St. Patrick's Day generates elevated foot traffic in Harlem, particularly in the afternoon. The neighbourhood has active Irish-American and community traditions around the holiday that drive dining demand.
NYC Spring Restaurant Week4/10
Mar 16, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide / Manhattan · Lunch
NYC Restaurant Week (Mar 16–28) drives heightened dining consideration across the city. While high-end restaurants see the direct benefit, the heightened dining mindset and increased foot traffic in commercial areas creates an above-baseline halo effect for all nearby QSR options — particularly for lunch and post-work dinner.

$10 Tuesdays: Eight-piece bucket or box of dark meat chicken or tenders for $10, available only on Tuesdays.
KFC · 0.02 mi
2026-02-24 | Product / Menu Changes
Queso Dip side launched for $1, designed to pair with large Potato Wedges orders.
KFC · 0.02 mi
2026-02-24 | Product / Menu Changes, Promotion
$5 Bowls menu introduced in 2026 with five varieties including Spicy Mashed Potato Bowl, Mac & Cheese Bowl (a comeback item), and Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl (a poutine-style dish with fries, gravy, chicken nuggets, and cheese curds).
KFC · 0.02 mi
2026-01-01 to 2026-02-28 | Loyalty & Digital Engagement, Promotion
"Sundays by KFC" digital-only deals exclusive to KFC Rewards members ran through January and February 2026, featuring rotating weekly specials including individual meals and family buckets.
McDonald's · 0.02 mi
2026-02-28 | Promotion
Free medium fries with $1 purchase every Friday throughout 2025
Burger King · 0.02 mi
2026-02-24 | Product / Menu Changes, Promotion
The fan-favorite Maple Bourbon BBQ Whopper returned on 2026-02-24 for a limited time. It includes a ¼ lb flame-grilled beef patty with maple bourbon BBQ sauce, crispy onions, jalapeños, smoky maple candied bacon, and American cheese on a toasted sesame seed bun.

Apr 02, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide

Mar 20, 2026 · 08:00 · The Bronx · All-Day
The Bronx has a sizeable Muslim community, particularly in the South Bronx and Fordham areas. Eid al-Fitr drives elevated family dining demand throughout the day.

Good Friday sees many office closures, school breaks, and early dismissals across NYC. Midday and afternoon dining demand is elevated as residents and families are home or in retail areas rather than at work.

St. Patrick's Day Parade
Mar 17, 2026 · 11:00 · Staten Island
Impact: 6/10 All-Day Cap: 150,000 Parade
Staten Island has a large Irish-American community and hosts its own St. Patrick's Day parades and events. Dining demand at the mall and surrounding area is notably elevated throughout the day.
Spring Break (NYC Schools)6/10
Apr 02, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
NYC public school spring break increases family dining demand across all neighbourhood locations. More families active mid-day creates above-average lunch and afternoon traffic for the full week.
Good Friday5/10
Apr 03, 2026 · 12:00 · Citywide · Lunch
Good Friday sees many office closures, school breaks, and early dismissals across NYC. Midday and afternoon dining demand is elevated as residents and families are home or in retail areas rather than at work.

⚙
Methodology & AI Pipeline
How Propheus agents built this report — fully automated, multi-stage intelligence pipeline

Pipeline Architecture

This report was generated by a multi-agent AI system that orchestrates spatial analysis, web intelligence gathering, and scoring computation in a fully automated pipeline. No manual data entry — every number is computed from source data and every claim is traced to its origin.

191
Events Analyzed
64
Brands Monitored
191
Promos Discovered
14 days
Weather Outlook
1
Spatial Data Ingestion
Agent: Places Discovery Agent • Source: Propheus Places
Ingested NYC points-of-interest with coordinates, ratings, review counts, categories, and business attributes across all 13 Chick-fil-A store trade areas.
2
Trade Area Computation
Applied practical trade distance for each store considering retail density in the neighbourhood, transit connectivity, and anchor locations. Each store is scored across 13 aggregate dimensions and 5 composite indices calibrated to the QSR context.
3
AI Classification & Brand Matching
AI Agents identify and classify QSR and fast-casual competitors for each store, understanding competitive relationships across direct rivals, adjacent categories, and delivery-only vs. walk-in concepts. Ghost kitchens and non-walk-in brands are excluded from threat ranking to ensure walk-in relevance.
4
Threat & Synergy Scoring (v2)
Engine: Scoring Engine v2 • Scale: All scores 0–100
Each competitor scored on: distance decay (0–40) + review volume log-scale (0–25) + rating (0–20) + promo intensity (0–15). 13 aggregate scores computed per store, then rolled into 5 composite scores using weighted formulas.
Competition Pressure Direct Competitor Threat Event Amplification Anchor Ecosystem Foot Traffic Proxy Tourism Transit Connectivity Dining Density
5
Promo Discovery Engine
Agent: Web Intelligence Agent • Coverage: 64 brands, 191 promos found
AI Agents autonomously discover and extract active promotions across competitor brands from multiple web sources. Each promotion is validated, structured, and mapped to the most relevant store location to surface timely competitive intelligence. Competitor Pressure Score (CPS) is computed per brand per store using EDP, W-SKU overlap, friction, recurrence, and distance decay.
191 Promos 64 Brands CPS Ranked per Store 191 Source URLs
6
Event Intelligence Engine
Agent: Event Intelligence Agent • Coverage: 930 events across 13 stores
AI Agents discover and score upcoming events across all NYC store trade areas. Each event is scored 1–10 on expected foot-traffic impact using proximity, venue capacity, meal window alignment, audience affinity, and recurrence. Events below score 3 are suppressed when higher-quality events exist. Supplemental citywide events (holidays, parades) are injected at store-specific relevance scores.
191 Events Proximity Scoring Meal Window Alignment Audience Affinity
7
Weather & Seasonal Impact Modeling
Horizon: 14-day rolling forecast • Signals: Temperature • Precipitation • Wind Speed
Each alert is scored against two baselines: historical averages from the prior 2 years and the trailing 14-day mean, with hourly anomalies layered on top for intraday precision. The Weather Agent interprets the direction and magnitude of each deviation in context — factoring in product category sensitivity, store operating hours, and whether the day falls on a weekday or weekend — to produce a single signed impact score per alert.
2-Year Historical Baseline 14-Day Rolling Mean Hourly Anomalies Category-Level Impact Operating Hours Context Weekday / Weekend Weighting

Chick-fil-A NYC Retail Intelligence Report • Powered by Propheus Retail Agent • March 2026
